message:
Add beads for external session support and reliable tmux commands

Created two P1 beads:
- bd-clpmz: Support external tmux sessions not created by ntm
- bd-16dhv: Use /usr/bin/tmux full path for reliable Enter key sending

Also adds swarm_setup.sh script for creating multi-project agent swarms.

The core issue: zsh tmux plugin intercepts bare `tmux` commands, causing
send-keys to fail silently. Fix is to use full path /usr/bin/tmux.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-168bh","title":"Unit Tests: Watch mode loop and exit conditions","description":"## Test Coverage
Unit tests for watch mode loop and exit conditions.

## Test Cases

### Watch Loop
- Initial assignment pass executes
- Completion detected triggers reassignment
- Unblocked beads detected and assigned
- Poll interval respected

### Exit Conditions
- Ctrl+C (SIGINT) triggers graceful shutdown
- --stop-when-done exits when no beads
- All agents crashed exits with error
- No more idle agents waits

### Streaming Output
- Timestamps accurate
- Assignment events logged
- Completion events logged
- Unblock events logged

### Error Handling
- BV unavailable mid-loop
- Agent Mail unavailable
- Assignment failure recovery

### Edge Cases
- Zero beads to start
- All beads blocked
- Single agent scenario
- Very fast completions

## Logging
- Log each loop iteration
- Log event processing
- Log exit reason

## Test File

 {"id":"bd-16a9","title":"Add built-in prompt library with common patterns (marching-orders, code-review, self-review, cross-review)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:32:23.221603857Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T10:17:06.327311020Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T10:17:06.327220830Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-16dhv","title":"Use /usr/bin/tmux full path for reliable Enter key sending","description":"## Problem

When running tmux send-keys commands from certain shell environments (particularly when zsh tmux plugin is active), the bare `tmux` command gets intercepted by shell functions/aliases, causing:
- Commands fail silently or produce errors like `_zsh_tmux_plugin_run: command not found`
- Enter key not actually sent to panes
- Users see success messages but agents don't receive input

## Root Cause

The zsh tmux plugin defines functions that wrap tmux, and when running from within tmux (or from scripts sourced in zsh), these can interfere with direct tmux calls.

## Solution

Use the full path `/usr/bin/tmux` (or `$(which tmux)` resolved at startup) for ALL tmux operations throughout ntm, especially:
- `send-keys` commands
- `capture-pane` commands  
- Any command that needs to reliably communicate with tmux

## Files to Update

- All Go files that shell out to tmux
- Ensure tmux binary path is resolved once at startup and used consistently

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All tmux calls use full path (not bare `tmux`)
- [ ] Works correctly in zsh with tmux plugin active
- [ ] Works from within tmux sessions
- [ ] Enter key reliably sent to all panes
- [ ] Add test to verify tmux path resolution","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-22T01:05:24.907919887Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T01:07:03.455861078Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-16ptx","title":"Task: --robot-is-working Command Implementation","description":"## Task: --robot-is-working Command Implementation

### Why This Command Exists

This is the DIRECT ANSWER to the users critical requirement:

> "NEVER interrupt agents doing useful work\!\!\!"

Before ANY restart action, a controller agent must be able to ask: "Is this agent actively working?" This command provides that answer with structured, actionable output.

### Command Specification

```bash
# Check single pane
ntm --robot-is-working=myproject --panes=2

# Check multiple panes
ntm --robot-is-working=myproject --panes=2,3,4

# Check all agent panes (excludes pane 1 control shell)
ntm --robot-is-working=myproject --panes=all

# With verbosity (include raw sample for debugging)
ntm --robot-is-working=myproject --panes=2 --verbose
```

### Output Format (JSON)

```json
{
  "session": "myproject",
  "timestamp": "2026-01-20T15:30:00Z",
  "query": {
    "panes_requested": [2, 3, 4],
    "lines_captured": 100
  },
  "panes": {
    "2": {
      "agent_type": "cc",
      "is_working": true,
      "is_idle": false,
      "is_rate_limited": false,
      "is_context_low": false,
      "context_remaining": null,
      "confidence": 0.95,
      "indicators": {
        "work": ["```", "Writing to ", "Created "],
        "limit": []
      },
      "recommendation": "DO_NOT_INTERRUPT",
      "recommendation_reason": "Agent is actively writing code"
    },
    "3": {
      "agent_type": "cod",
      "is_working": false,
      "is_idle": true,
      "is_rate_limited": false,
      "is_context_low": true,
      "context_remaining": 12.0,
      "confidence": 0.90,
      "indicators": {
        "work": [],
        "limit": []
      },
      "recommendation": "SAFE_TO_RESTART",
      "recommendation_reason": "Agent idle with low context (12%)"
    },
    "4": {
      "agent_type": "gmi",
      "is_working": false,
      "is_idle": false,
      "is_rate_limited": true,
      "is_context_low": false,
      "context_remaining": null,
      "confidence": 0.85,
      "indicators": {
        "work": [],
        "limit": ["rate limit", "quota exceeded"]
      },
      "recommendation": "RATE_LIMITED_WAIT",
      "recommendation_reason": "Agent hit rate limit"
    }
  },
  "summary": {
    "total_panes": 3,
    "working_count": 1,
    "idle_count": 1,
    "rate_limited_count": 1,
    "context_low_count": 1,
    "by_recommendation": {
      "DO_NOT_INTERRUPT": [2],
      "SAFE_TO_RESTART": [3],
      "RATE_LIMITED_WAIT": [4]
    }
  }
}
```

### Recommendation Values and Their Meaning

| Recommendation | Meaning | Controller Action |
|----------------|---------|-------------------|
| `DO_NOT_INTERRUPT` | Agent is actively producing output | Leave alone\! |
| `SAFE_TO_RESTART` | Agent is idle, can be restarted | Safe to send exit + new prompt |
| `CONTEXT_LOW_CONTINUE` | Working but low context | Let finish current task, then restart |
| `RATE_LIMITED_WAIT` | Hit usage limit | Wait for reset or switch account |
| `ERROR_STATE` | Agent in error state | Investigate before action |
| `UNKNOWN` | Could not determine state | Manual inspection needed |

### Implementation Flow

```go
package robot

import (
    "github.com/username/ntm/internal/agent"
)

type IsWorkingResult struct {
    Session   string                     `json:"session"`
    Timestamp string                     `json:"timestamp"`
    Query     IsWorkingQuery             `json:"query"`
    Panes     map[string]PaneWorkStatus  `json:"panes"`
    Summary   IsWorkingSummary           `json:"summary"`
}

type IsWorkingQuery struct {
    PanesRequested []int `json:"panes_requested"`
    LinesCaptured  int   `json:"lines_captured"`
}

type PaneWorkStatus struct {
    AgentType            string            `json:"agent_type"`
    IsWorking            bool              `json:"is_working"`
    IsIdle               bool              `json:"is_idle"`
    IsRateLimited        bool              `json:"is_rate_limited"`
    IsContextLow         bool              `json:"is_context_low"`
    ContextRemaining     *float64          `json:"context_remaining,omitempty"`
    Confidence           float64           `json:"confidence"`
    Indicators           WorkIndicators    `json:"indicators"`
    Recommendation       string            `json:"recommendation"`
    RecommendationReason string            `json:"recommendation_reason"`
    RawSample            string            `json:"raw_sample,omitempty"` // Only with --verbose
}

func IsWorking(session string, panes []int, verbose bool) (*IsWorkingResult, error) {
    result := &IsWorkingResult{
        Session:   session,
        Timestamp: time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
        Query: IsWorkingQuery{
            PanesRequested: panes,
            LinesCaptured:  100, // configurable
        },
        Panes: make(map[string]PaneWorkStatus),
    }
    
    parser := agent.NewParser()
    
    for _, pane := range panes {
        // Step 1: Capture pane output
        output, err := capturePaneOutput(session, pane, 100)
        if err \!= nil {
            // Record error but continue with other panes
            result.Panes[strconv.Itoa(pane)] = PaneWorkStatus{
                Recommendation:       "ERROR_STATE",
                RecommendationReason: fmt.Sprintf("Failed to capture: %v", err),
            }
            continue
        }
        
        // Step 2: Parse with agent parser
        state, err := parser.Parse(output)
        if err \!= nil {
            result.Panes[strconv.Itoa(pane)] = PaneWorkStatus{
                Recommendation:       "UNKNOWN",
                RecommendationReason: fmt.Sprintf("Parse failed: %v", err),
            }
            continue
        }
        
        // Step 3: Build status
        status := PaneWorkStatus{
            AgentType:        string(state.Type),
            IsWorking:        state.IsWorking,
            IsIdle:           state.IsIdle,
            IsRateLimited:    state.IsRateLimited,
            IsContextLow:     state.IsContextLow,
            ContextRemaining: state.ContextRemaining,
            Confidence:       state.Confidence,
            Indicators: WorkIndicators{
                Work:  state.WorkIndicators,
                Limit: state.LimitIndicators,
            },
            Recommendation:       string(state.GetRecommendation()),
            RecommendationReason: getRecommendationReason(state),
        }
        
        if verbose {
            status.RawSample = state.RawSample
        }
        
        result.Panes[strconv.Itoa(pane)] = status
    }
    
    // Step 4: Build summary
    result.Summary = buildSummary(result.Panes)
    
    return result, nil
}

func getRecommendationReason(state *agent.AgentState) string {
    rec := state.GetRecommendation()
    switch rec {
    case agent.RecommendDoNotInterrupt:
        return "Agent is actively producing output"
    case agent.RecommendSafeToRestart:
        return "Agent is idle"
    case agent.RecommendContextLowContinue:
        return fmt.Sprintf("Working but low context (%.0f%%)", *state.ContextRemaining)
    case agent.RecommendRateLimitedWait:
        return "Agent hit rate limit"
    case agent.RecommendErrorState:
        return "Agent in error state"
    default:
        return "Could not determine agent state"
    }
}
```

### Integration with Existing NTM Infrastructure

This command builds on existing robot mode infrastructure:

```go
// Uses existing --robot-tail to capture output
func capturePaneOutput(session string, pane int, lines int) (string, error) {
    // Internally calls same logic as:
    // ntm --robot-tail=session --panes=pane --lines=lines
    return robot.TailPane(session, pane, lines)
}
```

### Files to Create/Modify

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `cmd/ntm/robot_is_working.go` | CLI flag handling, arg parsing |
| `internal/robot/is_working.go` | Core logic (above) |
| `internal/robot/is_working_test.go` | Unit tests |

### Exit Codes

| Code | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| 0 | All panes analyzed successfully |
| 1 | Some panes could not be analyzed |
| 2 | Session not found |
| 3 | Invalid arguments |

### Usage Examples for Controllers

```bash
# Controller workflow: Check before restart
result=$(ntm --robot-is-working=myproject --panes=2)
recommendation=$(echo "$result" | jq -r ".panes.\"2\".recommendation")

if [ "$recommendation" = "DO_NOT_INTERRUPT" ]; then
    echo "Agent is working, leaving alone"
elif [ "$recommendation" = "SAFE_TO_RESTART" ]; then
    echo "Safe to restart"
    ntm --robot-smart-restart=myproject --panes=2
elif [ "$recommendation" = "RATE_LIMITED_WAIT" ]; then
    echo "Rate limited, waiting"
    # Could trigger caam account switch here
fi
```

### Why This Must Be Done Before Smart Restart

`--robot-smart-restart` (bd-2c7f4) DEPENDS on this command. The smart restart command calls `--robot-is-working` internally before taking any action. Without this foundation, smart restart cannot be implemented.

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Command accepts session and panes arguments
- [ ] Returns structured JSON per specification
- [ ] Correctly identifies working vs idle agents
- [ ] Provides confidence scores
- [ ] Generates actionable recommendations with reasons
- [ ] Exit codes match specification
- [ ] Integrates with parser module (bd-2iw6e)
- [ ] Unit tests cover all agent types and states","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-20T21:37:16.367320719Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T02:04:26.860294212Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T02:04:26.860232195Z","close_reason":"Implemented --robot-is-working command with full JSON output including session, query, per-pane status (agent type, working/idle/rate-limited/context-low, confidence, indicators, recommendation), and summary stats. Integrates with agent parser from bd-2iw6e.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-16ptx","depends_on_id":"bd-1bdxp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-20T21:37:16.393784178Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-16ptx","depends_on_id":"bd-2iw6e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-20T21:37:38.851027169Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1s6cs","title":"TOON: Add --robot-output-format flag to all robot commands","description":"Add output format flag to the robot command system.

## Approach

Robot commands share a common infrastructure. Add format flag at the top level so all commands inherit it.

## Flag Definition

```go
var robotOutputFormat string

func init() {
    rootCmd.PersistentFlags().StringVar(&robotOutputFormat, 
        "robot-output-format", 
        "json",
        "Output format for robot commands (json, toon)")
}
```

## Integration with Serializer

```go
func getRobotSerializer() serialize.Serializer {
    format := serialize.Format(robotOutputFormat)
    if format == "" {
        format = serialize.Format(config.Robot.Output.Format)
    }
    return serialize.NewSerializer(format)
}
```

## Update Robot Commands

All robot commands that output data need to use the serializer:

```go
func robotListPanesCmd(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
    panes := listPanes()
    serializer := getRobotSerializer()
    output, err := serializer.Serialize(panes)
    if err != nil {
        return err
    }
    fmt.Println(string(output))
    return nil
}
```

## Code Location
- internal/robot/flags.go (new)
- Update all files in internal/robot/

## Testing

- Test each robot command with both formats","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:04:03.960119816Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T07:05:55.013161354Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1s6cs","depends_on_id":"bd-1zrjo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:05:55.012291175Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1s6cs","depends_on_id":"bd-6wc0a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:04:04.010614614Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1s9b","title":"Task: Implement proficiency config storage","description":"## Overview
Create configuration module for storing and retrieving user proficiency tier.

## Deliverables
1. Create internal/config/proficiency.go:
   - ProficiencyConfig struct with Tier, UsageStats, PromotionHistory
   - Load() - read from ~/.config/ntm/proficiency.json
   - Save() - persist to config file
   - GetTier() - return current tier (env override > config > default)

2. Configuration file format (proficiency.json):
   {
     "tier": 1,
     "usage_stats": {
       "commands_run": 150,
       "sessions_created": 25,
       "prompts_sent": 200,
       "first_use": "2025-01-01T00:00:00Z",
       "last_use": "2025-01-15T00:00:00Z"
     },
     "promotion_history": [
       {"from": 1, "to": 2, "at": "2025-01-10T00:00:00Z", "reason": "manual"}
     ]
   }

3. Environment override:
   - NTM_PROFICIENCY_TIER=1|2|3 overrides config
   - Useful for scripts that need specific tier behavior

4. Default behavior:
   - New users start at Tier 1
   - Create config file on first use

## Implementation Notes
- Use os.UserConfigDir() for cross-platform config path
- Handle missing/corrupted config gracefully (reset to defaults)
- Log tier changes for debugging

 {"id":"bd-1sviv","title":"Unit Tests: All ntm assign strategies (balanced, speed, quality, dependency)","description":"## Test Coverage
Test all ntm assign strategy algorithms besides round-robin.

## Test Cases

### Balanced Strategy
- Distribute evenly when all agents have 0 assignments
- Prefer agents with fewer current assignments
- Handle tie-breaking (use pane order)
- Update scores after each assignment

### Speed Strategy
- Prefer agents with lower context usage
- Score = 1.0 - (context_pct / 100)
- Handle agents at 100% context (score = 0)
- Prefer fresh agents over loaded ones

### Quality Strategy
- Match bead type to agent capabilities
- Claude for refactoring/architecture
- Codex for feature/bug_fix
- Gemini for documentation/review
- Handle unknown bead types (default score)

### Dependency Strategy
- Prioritize high PageRank beads
- Prioritize high unblocks_count beads
- Score = pagerank * 0.5 + unblocks * 0.5
- Sort beads by score before assignment

### Strategy Selection
- Default to balanced
- Parse --strategy flag correctly
- Error on unknown strategy

## Detailed Logging Requirements (Go test)

### Test Structure
```go
func TestBalancedStrategy(t *testing.T) {
    t.Log("=== TestBalancedStrategy ===")
    
    // Setup
    t.Log("[SETUP] Creating test agents and beads")
    agents := []Agent{...}
    beads := []Bead{...}
    t.Logf("[SETUP] Agents: %d, Beads: %d", len(agents), len(beads))
    
    // Execute
    t.Log("[EXEC] Running balanced strategy")
    result := BalancedStrategy(beads, agents)
    t.Logf("[EXEC] Result: %d assignments", len(result))
    
    // Verify
    t.Log("[VERIFY] Checking distribution")
    for i, a := range result {
        t.Logf("[VERIFY] Assignment %d: bead=%s -> pane=%d, score=%.2f",
            i, a.BeadID, a.Pane, a.Score)
    }
    
    // Assert
    if len(result) != len(beads) {
        t.Errorf("[FAIL] Expected %d assignments, got %d", len(beads), len(result))
    } else {
        t.Log("[PASS] Assignment count correct")
    }
}
```

### Log Points per Test

#### Setup Logging
```go
t.Logf("[SETUP] Test: %s", t.Name())
t.Logf("[SETUP] Input agents: %+v", agents)
t.Logf("[SETUP] Input beads: %+v", beads)
t.Logf("[SETUP] Options: %+v", opts)
```

#### Execution Logging
```go
t.Log("[EXEC] Calling strategy function")
t.Logf("[EXEC] Strategy: %s", strategyName)
t.Logf("[EXEC] Duration: %v", elapsed)
```

#### Scoring Logging (verbose)
```go
t.Logf("[SCORE] Bead %s:", bead.ID)
for _, agent := range agents {
    t.Logf("[SCORE]   Agent %d (%s): score=%.2f", 
        agent.Pane, agent.Type, score)
}
t.Logf("[SCORE] Winner: pane %d with score %.2f", winner.Pane, winner.Score)
```

#### Assertion Logging
```go
// Before assertion
t.Logf("[ASSERT] Checking: %s", description)
t.Logf("[ASSERT] Expected: %v", expected)
t.Logf("[ASSERT] Actual: %v", actual)

// Result
if expected == actual {
    t.Logf("[PASS] %s", description)
} else {
    t.Errorf("[FAIL] %s: expected %v, got %v", description, expected, actual)
}
```

### Table-Driven Tests with Logging
```go
func TestStrategyScoring(t *testing.T) {
    tests := []struct {
        name     string
        strategy string
        agents   []Agent
        beads    []Bead
        want     []Assignment
    }{
        {"balanced_even", "balanced", ...},
        {"balanced_uneven", "balanced", ...},
        {"speed_fresh", "speed", ...},
    }
    
    for _, tt := range tests {
        t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
            t.Logf("[TEST] %s", tt.name)
            t.Logf("[INPUT] Strategy=%s, Agents=%d, Beads=%d",
                tt.strategy, len(tt.agents), len(tt.beads))
            
            got := RunStrategy(tt.strategy, tt.beads, tt.agents)
            
            t.Logf("[OUTPUT] Assignments: %d", len(got))
            for i, a := range got {
                t.Logf("[OUTPUT] [%d] bead=%s pane=%d score=%.2f",
                    i, a.BeadID, a.Pane, a.Score)
            }
            
            if !reflect.DeepEqual(got, tt.want) {
                t.Errorf("[FAIL] Mismatch\nwant: %+v\ngot:  %+v", tt.want, got)
            } else {
                t.Log("[PASS] Output matches expected")
            }
        })
    }
}
```

### Running with Verbose Output
```bash
go test -v -run TestBalancedStrategy ./internal/assign/...
```

Automatically register each spawned agent with Agent Mail for inter-agent communication.

for _, pane := range panes {
    if pane.Type == AgentUser { continue }

        ProjectKey:      s.projectDir,
        Program:         'ntm',
        Model:           pane.Variant,
        TaskDescription: fmt.Sprintf('Agent in pane %s', pane.Title),
    })
    
    s.agentRegistry[pane.ID] = agent.Name
}

- Mapping stored for message routing
- Graceful handling when Agent Mail unavailable
- Registration survives session restart

 {"id":"bd-1t8p","title":"EPIC: Local LLM Support via Ollama and llama.cpp","description":"## Overview
Add support for local LLM backends (Ollama, llama.cpp, LM Studio) as first-class agent types, enabling privacy-sensitive work, offline operation, and cost-free experimentation.

## Problem Statement
Current NTM only supports cloud LLM providers (Claude, OpenAI, Gemini). Users need:
- Privacy for sensitive codebases (air-gapped environments)
- Offline operation when internet unavailable
- Cost-free experimentation and learning
- Running specialized/fine-tuned local models

## Key Features
1. New agent type: --local=N or --ollama=N for spawning local agents
2. Model selection: --local-model=codellama:34b
3. Mixed fleets: combine cloud and local agents in same session
4. Performance monitoring: track token/s, memory usage
5. Automatic model management: pull models on demand

## Technical Design
1. Ollama adapter:
   - Connect to local Ollama server (default: localhost:11434)
   - Map NTM commands to Ollama API
   - Handle streaming responses

2. llama.cpp adapter:
   - Direct integration for llama.cpp server mode
   - Support GGUF model loading
   - GPU acceleration detection

3. Agent type hierarchy:
   - CloudAgent (existing): Claude, Codex, Gemini
   - LocalAgent (new): Ollama, LlamaCpp
   - All agents implement same interface

## Configuration
- NTM_OLLAMA_HOST for custom Ollama URL
- NTM_LOCAL_MODEL for default model
- Per-project config in ntm.yaml

## Success Criteria
- Can spawn local agents alongside cloud agents
- Local agents participate in normal workflows
- Performance metrics visible in dashboard
- Works fully offline (after model cached)

## Files Likely Affected
- internal/agent/local.go (new - local agent implementation)
- internal/agent/ollama.go (new - Ollama adapter)
- internal/cli/spawn.go (add --local flags)
- internal/tui/dashboard/panels/agents.go (show local agent metrics)","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

+{"id":"bd-clpmz","title":"Support external tmux sessions not created by ntm","description":"## Problem

When users create tmux sessions manually (not via `ntm spawn`), ntm commands like `ntm send`, `ntm list`, `ntm status` have issues:
- `ntm send` tries to use pane numbering that assumes ntm's convention (session:N)
- Session metadata is missing (agent types, project mapping)
- Cannot properly target panes by agent type (--cc, --cod, --gmi)

## Example

User creates sessions manually:
```bash
tmux new-session -d -s cc_agents_1
# ... creates 12 panes with cc running in each
```

Then tries to use ntm:
```bash
ntm send cc_agents_1 --cc 'Hello'  # Doesn't work properly
```

## Proposed Solution

1. Add `ntm adopt <session>` command to register external sessions
2. Allow specifying agent type mapping during adoption
3. Store metadata in a way that persists across restarts
4. Make `ntm list` show adopted sessions with a marker
5. Full functionality (send, status, copy, etc.) should work with adopted sessions

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `ntm adopt <session> [--cc=PANES] [--cod=PANES] [--gmi=PANES]` command
- [ ] Adopted sessions appear in `ntm list`
- [ ] `ntm send` works correctly with adopted sessions
- [ ] `ntm status` shows pane details for adopted sessions
- [ ] Documentation updated","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-22T01:04:51.600762438Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T01:06:29.024049209Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -0,0 +1,184 @@
+#!/bin/bash
+# swarm_setup.sh - Create tmux sessions with agent panes for multi-project work
+# Usage: ./swarm_setup.sh [--launch]
+#   --launch: Also start the agents (cc, cod, gmi) in each pane
+
+set -euo pipefail
+
+LAUNCH_AGENTS=false
+if [[ "${1:-}" == "--launch" ]]; then
+    LAUNCH_AGENTS=true
+fi
+
+# Kill existing tmux server
+tmux kill-server 2>/dev/null || true
+sleep 1
+
+PANES_PER_SESSION=12
+
+# Session names
+CC_SESSIONS=(cc_agents_1 cc_agents_2 cc_agents_3)
+COD_SESSIONS=(cod_agents_1 cod_agents_2 cod_agents_3)
+GMI_SESSIONS=(gmi_agents_1 gmi_agents_2 gmi_agents_3)
+
+# Project assignments with agent counts
+# Format: "project_name:cc_count:cod_count:gmi_count"
+PROJECTS=(
+    "flywheel_connectors:4:4:2"
+    "wezterm_automata:3:3:2"
+    "asupersync:3:3:2"
+    "ntm:3:3:2"
+    "jeffreysprompts_premium:3:3:2"
+    "jeffreys-skills.md:3:3:2"
+    "rust_stream_deck:1:1:1"
+    "fastapi_rust:1:1:1"
+    "coding_agent_usage_tracker:1:1:1"
+    "process_triage:1:1:1"
+    "coding_agent_account_manager:1:1:1"
+    "rust_proxy:1:1:1"
+    "remote_compilation_helper:1:1:1"
+    "destructive_command_guard:1:1:1"
+    "flywheel_gateway:1:1:1"
+    "meta_skill:1:1:1"
+    "agent_settings_backup_script:1:1:1"
+    "coding_agent_session_search:1:1:1"
+    "sqlmodel_rust:1:1:1"
+    "rano:1:1:1"
+    "agentic_coding_flywheel_setup:1:1:1"
+)
+
+# Build flat arrays of pane assignments
+declare -a CC_PANES=()
+declare -a COD_PANES=()
+declare -a GMI_PANES=()
+
+for proj_spec in "${PROJECTS[@]}"; do
+    IFS=':' read -r proj cc_count cod_count gmi_count <<< "$proj_spec"
+    for ((i=0; i<cc_count; i++)); do
+        CC_PANES+=("$proj")
+    done
+    for ((i=0; i<cod_count; i++)); do
+        COD_PANES+=("$proj")
+    done
+    for ((i=0; i<gmi_count; i++)); do
+        GMI_PANES+=("$proj")
+    done
+done
+
+# Pad arrays to fill all panes
+while ((${#CC_PANES[@]} < PANES_PER_SESSION * 3)); do
+    CC_PANES+=("EMPTY")
+done
+while ((${#COD_PANES[@]} < PANES_PER_SESSION * 3)); do
+    COD_PANES+=("EMPTY")
+done
+while ((${#GMI_PANES[@]} < PANES_PER_SESSION * 3)); do
+    GMI_PANES+=("EMPTY")
+done
+
+echo "CC agents: 34 (+2 empty), COD agents: 34 (+2 empty), GMI agents: 27 (+9 empty)"
+
+# Create a session with 12 panes using simple sequential splitting
+create_session_with_panes() {
+    local session_name=$1
+
+    # Create session with first window
+    tmux new-session -d -s "$session_name" -x 320 -y 80
+
+    # Add 11 more panes by splitting (total = 12)
+    for ((i=1; i<12; i++)); do
+        # Always split the first pane, tmux will rebalance
+        tmux split-window -t "${session_name}"
+        tmux select-layout -t "${session_name}" tiled
+    done
+
+    # Final tiled layout
+    tmux select-layout -t "${session_name}" tiled
+}
+
+# Setup a specific pane with project and agent
+setup_pane() {
+    local session=$1
+    local pane_idx=$2
+    local project=$3
+    local agent_cmd=$4  # cc, cod, or gmi
+
+    local target="${session}:1.${pane_idx}"
+
+    if [[ "$project" != "EMPTY" ]]; then
+        # Change to project directory
+        tmux send-keys -t "$target" "cd /dp/${project} && clear" Enter
+        sleep 0.02
+
+        if [[ "$LAUNCH_AGENTS" == "true" ]]; then
+            sleep 0.1
+            tmux send-keys -t "$target" "$agent_cmd" Enter
+        fi
+    else
+        tmux send-keys -t "$target" "echo 'Empty pane - available for assignment'" Enter
+    fi
+}
+
+# Create all sessions
+echo "Creating sessions..."
+for session in "${CC_SESSIONS[@]}" "${COD_SESSIONS[@]}" "${GMI_SESSIONS[@]}"; do
+    echo "  Creating $session..."
+    create_session_with_panes "$session"
+done
+
+# Helper to track instances
+declare -A cc_instance=()
+declare -A cod_instance=()
+declare -A gmi_instance=()
+
+# Assign CC panes
+echo "Assigning CC panes..."
+for session_idx in 0 1 2; do
+    session="${CC_SESSIONS[$session_idx]}"
+    for pane_idx in $(seq 1 12); do
+        global_idx=$((session_idx * 12 + pane_idx - 1))
+        proj="${CC_PANES[$global_idx]}"
+        setup_pane "$session" "$pane_idx" "$proj" "cc"
+    done
+done
+
+# Assign COD panes
+echo "Assigning COD panes..."
+for session_idx in 0 1 2; do
+    session="${COD_SESSIONS[$session_idx]}"
+    for pane_idx in $(seq 1 12); do
+        global_idx=$((session_idx * 12 + pane_idx - 1))
+        proj="${COD_PANES[$global_idx]}"
+        setup_pane "$session" "$pane_idx" "$proj" "cod"
+    done
+done
+
+# Assign GMI panes
+echo "Assigning GMI panes..."
+for session_idx in 0 1 2; do
+    session="${GMI_SESSIONS[$session_idx]}"
+    for pane_idx in $(seq 1 12); do
+        global_idx=$((session_idx * 12 + pane_idx - 1))
+        proj="${GMI_PANES[$global_idx]}"
+        setup_pane "$session" "$pane_idx" "$proj" "gmi"
+    done
+done
+
+echo ""
+echo "=== SWARM SETUP COMPLETE ==="
+echo "Sessions created:"
+tmux list-sessions 2>/dev/null || echo "  (none)"
+echo ""
+echo "Pane layout (12 panes per session):"
+echo "  CC:  ${CC_SESSIONS[*]}"
+echo "  COD: ${COD_SESSIONS[*]}"
+echo "  GMI: ${GMI_SESSIONS[*]}"
+echo ""
+if [[ "$LAUNCH_AGENTS" == "true" ]]; then
+    echo "Agents launched in all panes."
+else
+    echo "To launch agents, run: $0 --launch"
+    echo "Or attach and manually run cc/cod/gmi in each pane."
+fi
+echo ""
